display.purpose: Page-level titles and authored workspace statements.

headline.purpose: Section anchors across workspace panels and analysis blocks.

body.purpose: Default reading rhythm for operational copy and metadata.

label.purpose: Uppercase instrumentation, badges, chips, and control labels.

shadows.name: ambient-low
shadows.purpose: Default panel lift over the parchment background.

shadows.name: ambient-high
shadows.purpose: Primary structure surfaces such as the workspace app bar.

shadows.name: action-lift
shadows.purpose: Primary action emphasis on ember buttons.

motion.name: workspace-rise
motion.purpose: Initial entrance for major workspace sections.

motion.name: action-standard
motion.purpose: Default feedback timing for buttons and interactive controls.

motion.name: micro-standard
motion.purpose: Compact state shifts for metric buttons and smaller UI affordances.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Decisive action for search, import, export, and commit moments.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--cs-accent, #8B3F29); color: var(--cs-cream-text, #FFFAF1); padding: 0.74rem 1.1rem; border: 0; border-radius: 999px; font-family: "Familjen Grotesk", "Segoe UI", sans-serif; font-size: 0.76rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; box-shadow: 0 12px 24px rgba(139, 63, 41, 0.18); transition: transform 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease, filter 180ms ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 18px 32px rgba(139, 63, 41, 0.22); background: #A7623B; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.26); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Support action with tactile neutrality and clear hover lift.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.8); color: var(--cs-panel-title, #19352C); padding: 0.68rem 0.96rem; border: 1px solid rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.14); border-radius: 999px; font-family: "Familjen Grotesk", "Segoe UI", sans-serif; font-size: 0.76rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; transition: transform 180ms ease, border-color 180ms ease, background-color 180ms ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); border-color: rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.24); background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.92); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.26); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: translateY(0); }

components.name: Search Field
components.description: Workbench input with soft inset highlight and clear focus state.
components.css: .ds-input-wrap { display: grid; gap: 0.5rem; } .ds-input-label { color: var(--cs-muted, #5D685F); font-family: "Familjen Grotesk", "Segoe UI", sans-serif; font-size: 0.76rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-input-search { width: 100%; padding: 0.98rem 1.1rem; border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid rgba(33, 52, 43, 0.16); background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.78); color: var(--cs-ink, #14261F); font-family: "Familjen Grotesk", "Segoe UI", sans-serif; font-size: 0.96rem; box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.72); } .ds-input-search:hover { border-color: rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.22); } .ds-input-search:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.26); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Workspace Panel
components.description: The canonical work surface for search, analysis, and deck operations.
components.html:
Deck Signal

Bracket outlook

The panel stays warm and quiet so analysis states can do the talking.
components.css: .ds-panel { padding: 1.35rem; border-radius: 30px; border: 1px solid rgba(45, 60, 50, 0.14); background: rgba(251, 247, 239, 0.9); box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(19, 30, 24, 0.07); backdrop-filter: blur(18px); color: var(--cs-panel-title, #19352C); } .ds-panel h3 { margin: 0.25rem 0 0; font-family: "Literata", Georgia, serif; font-size: clamp(1.55rem, 2.4vw, 2.2rem); line-height: 1.1; letter-spacing: -0.03em; } .ds-panel p { margin: 0.55rem 0 0; color: var(--cs-muted, #5D685F); font-family: "Familjen Grotesk", "Segoe UI", sans-serif; font-size: 0.96rem; line-height: 1.5; } .ds-panel-label { margin: 0; color: var(--cs-muted, #5D685F); font-size: 0.76rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Theme Chip
components.description: Compact analysis marker for detected themes and confidence states.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.45rem; padding: 0.45rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(88, 116, 79, 0.1); color: #33442F; font-family: "Familjen Grotesk", "Segoe UI", sans-serif; font-size: 0.76rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-chip svg { width: 0.95rem; height: 0.95rem; } .ds-chip:hover { background: rgba(88, 116, 79, 0.16); } .ds-chip:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.26); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Deck App Bar
components.description: Rounded command rail for the deck title, state summary, and high-value actions.
components.html:
Synergy Sphere

Azorius Artifacts · Commander Set

100 cards
components.css: .ds-appbar { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 1rem; padding: 0.7rem 1rem; border-radius: 34px; border: 1px solid rgba(45, 60, 50, 0.2); background: linear-gradient(125deg, rgba(255, 250, 241, 0.82), rgba(249, 243, 231, 0.62)), linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.08), rgba(255, 255, 255, 0)); box-shadow: 0 22px 54px rgba(19, 30, 24, 0.1); color: var(--cs-panel-title, #19352C); } .ds-appbar-copy { display: grid; gap: 0.22rem; } .ds-appbar-copy h2 { margin: 0; font-family: "Literata", Georgia, serif; font-size: clamp(1.35rem, 2vw, 1.8rem); line-height: 1; letter-spacing: -0.03em; } .ds-appbar-copy p { margin: 0; color: var(--cs-muted, #5D685F); font-family: "Familjen Grotesk", "Segoe UI", sans-serif; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-appbar-actions { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; flex-wrap: wrap; } .ds-appbar-action { padding: 0.7rem 1rem; border: 1px solid rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.16); border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.88); color: var(--cs-panel-title, #19352C); font-family: "Familjen Grotesk", "Segoe UI", sans-serif; font-size: 0.76rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; box-shadow: 0 8px 18px rgba(22, 30, 24, 0.08); transition: transform 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease, border-color 180ms ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-appbar-action:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 14px 26px rgba(22, 30, 24, 0.12); } .ds-appbar-action:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.26); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-appbar-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.38rem 0.72rem; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(31, 91, 75, 0.12); color: var(--cs-panel-title, #19352C); font-family: "Familjen Grotesk", "Segoe UI", sans-serif; font-size: 0.82rem; font-weight: 700; } @media (max-width: 720px) { .ds-appbar { flex-direction: column; align-items: stretch; } .ds-appbar-action { width: 100%; } }

narrative.overview: CommandSynergy should feel like a deckbuilder's table that has already been arranged by someone with taste. The surface is bold, tactile, and strategically sharp: parchment light instead of sterile white, structured like an instrument instead of a dashboard, and warm enough to feel collectible without ever slipping into fantasy cosplay. The interface earns confidence through disciplined signal placement. Large radii, soft ambient layers, and archival neutrals keep the workspace approachable; rust, moss, and gilt appear only where the system needs to direct action, confirm state, or spotlight meaning. This is not a productivity template with card art dropped into it. It is a purpose-built analysis surface for Commander players. What the system rejects is explicit. It must not drift into generic SaaS or admin dashboard patterns, glossy futuristic styling, neutral dashboard chrome, filler surfaces, or generic productivity-tool cues. If a screen feels like clerical data entry instead of deliberate deck construction, it is wrong.
keyCharacteristics: Light-first, archival surfaces with visible material warmth.
Literary serif headlines over a sturdy grotesk body for collected authority.
Soft layering and blur used sparingly to separate work areas, not to decorate them.
Accent colors reserved for signals, actions, and analysis states.
Responsive behavior that collapses structure cleanly at 1180px, 920px, and 720px without changing the system's voice.

rules.name: The Signal Reserve Rule
rules.body: Ember Rust is scarce on purpose. If it starts appearing as decoration, the interface loses authority.

rules.name: The Counterweight Rule
rules.body: Moss Green and Gilt Brass support the system; they never compete with Ember Rust for primary attention on the same interaction.

rules.name: The Instrument Label Rule
rules.body: Small labels are always uppercase, tightly tracked, and semantically useful. If a label is decorative, remove it.

rules.name: The Serif Threshold Rule
rules.body: Serif typography marks structure and importance. Do not use it for buttons, filters, data tables, or dense utility copy.

rules.name: The Lift On Intent Rule
rules.body: Elevation appears when the interface asks for attention or action. Flat is the resting state.

rules.name: The Blur Has A Job Rule
rules.body: Backdrop blur is permitted only on overlays and drawers where it clarifies layering. It is forbidden as decorative frosting on ordinary panels.

dos: Do keep primary work surfaces on Linen Surface (#FBF7EF) or Vellum Surface (#FFFCF6) with large radii (22px to 30px) and soft ambient lift.
Do use Ember Rust (#8B3F29) for commitment moments only: primary actions, recovery actions, and destructive emphasis.
Do preserve uppercase label typography at 0.76rem with 0.18em tracking for badges, metadata, and interface instrumentation.
Do collapse layout structure cleanly at 1180px, 920px, and 720px; mobile should feel like the same workbench, not a separate minimal app.
Do keep focus visible with moss-tinted outlines and offsets. Keyboard state must remain obvious on every actionable control.
donts: Don't let the interface drift into generic SaaS or admin dashboard patterns. That includes anonymous white cards, interchangeable KPI tiles, and templated app-shell chrome.
Don't introduce glossy futuristic styling. No neon accents, no slick sci-fi gradients, no synthetic glow language.
Don't lean on neutral dashboard chrome. If a surface becomes gray, square, and emotionally blank, it is outside the system.
Don't add filler surfaces just to group content. Every panel, chip, or banner needs a job.
Don't borrow generic productivity-tool cues such as flat toolbar rows, generic tab strips, or monochrome utility panels that make deckbuilding feel like clerical data entry.
Don't use decorative blur, gradient text, colored side-stripe borders, or any other visual shorthand that reads as template work rather than a crafted Commander tool.
